attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who targets stage wins from early moves rather than late attacks. Excels on extremely steep gradients (24%+) where pure climbing power matters more than explosive acceleration.
team_tactics: Operates with minimal team support, relying on personal initiative to infiltrate breaks on stages where peloton control is relaxed or non-existent. Not set up for GC protection.
terrain_preference: Brutally steep mountain finishes where sustained power trumps punchy acceleration. Vulnerable on non-mountain stages where positioning errors prove costly.
vulnerability: Peloton positioning and crash avoidance in compressed situations. Early time losses in Grand Tours on moderately difficult stages where GC teams control pace but gradients don't suit his strengths.
key_patterns: Gains GC positions via breakaway infiltration on relaxed stages (2022 Tour, 2022 Vuelta wins).
Thrives on exceptionally steep gradients 20%+ where sustained climbing power decides outcome.
Positioning errors in bunch situations cost GC ambitions (2021 Vuelta crash, 2018 time loss).
Career resurgence after plateau: first GT stage win at age 30 (2022 Vuelta).
White jersey contender capabilities shown in 2017 Tour, strong young climber profile established early.
form_trajectory: positive: first Grand Tour stage win at age 30 on extreme gradients suggests tactical maturity, shifted from GC vulnerability to breakaway success model
